description_long: This is a single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a streamlined fuselage and a classic WWII-era fighter silhouette. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the midsection of the fuselage, with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral angles. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, and the landing gear appears to be a taildragger configuration with two main wheels and a small tail wheel. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and is enclosed, likely accommodating a single pilot. The exterior is finished in a combination of gray and darker gray tones, with a white marking on the tail and a band around the fuselage. The overall construction suggests a metal airframe, typical of mid-20th-century military aircraft.
description: A streamlined, single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a conventional tail and enclosed forward cockpit, finished in gray tones with white markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose intake, straight wings, and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, cockpit placement, and tail markings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the tail wheel.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the same features as the left, with clear visibility of the tail and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings, no winglets, and minimal dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main wheels and a single tail wheel, fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
